title: senecio integrifolius subsp. maritimus
intro: Senecio integrifolius subsp. maritimus, also known as Sea Groundsel, is a subspecies of the Senecio integrifolius plant, a flowering plant in the daisy family native to the coastal areas of the Mediterranean region. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Sea Groundsel include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Sea Groundsel is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from minor cuts and scrapes to more serious infections.
